edge_hypothesis: RSI pullback showed PF=1.164 with 51 trades (claude-rp6ht5a8) — a promising signal — but was REJECTED due to catastrophic DD=185%. Root cause: 1.0x ATR stop allows single trades to accumulate massive adverse excursion. The advisory explicitly recommends testing RSI pullback with stop >= 2.0x ATR. The signal mechanism (buy dips in EMA_200-confirmed uptrends when RSI shows temporary weakness) is fundamentally sound and DIFFERENT from MACD/supertrend — it enters AFTER pullbacks rather than at momentum shifts. By widening stops to 2.0-2.5x ATR, we give pullback entries room to recover without getting stopped out, while maintaining 1.6-2.0:1 R:R for fee drag management. The shallow pullback zone (RSI 42-45) produced trades while deep (RSI < 35) produced zero — so we keep RSI thresholds at 42-45 for longs. Regime assumption: TRENDING (EMA_200 slope filter baked into template). Invalidation: if DD > 25% on any variant despite 2.0x+ stops, the template's trend filter is insufficient for drawdown control and RSI pullback should be deprioritized.

variants.name: rsi_pullback_safe_moderate
variants.description: RSI pullback with 2.0x ATR stop (vs 1.0x that caused 185% DD) and 4.0x TP. Tests the advisory hypothesis that wider stops fix DD while preserving the PF=1.164 signal quality. R:R = 2:1. RSI threshold at 45 captures shallow pullbacks that generate enough trades. EMA_200 structural trend filter ensures we only buy dips in confirmed uptrends.
entry_long: EMA_50 slope is rising (EMA_50 > prev EMA_50) confirms intermediate momentum.
RSI_14 <= 45 identifies temporary pullback weakness in the trend.
entry_short: EMA_50 slope is falling (EMA_50 < prev EMA_50) confirms intermediate momentum.
RSI_14 >= 55 identifies temporary rally weakness in the downtrend.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Trend filter: Close must be above/below EMA_200 (structural trend confirmation)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (4.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: if wide stop still produces DD > 25%, the trend filter is too weak
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 4.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: RSI_14 pullback detection — shallow threshold (45) for trade frequency
EMA_200 structural trend + EMA_50 slope for intermediate momentum
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 4.0x TP sizing — DD fix variant

variants.name: rsi_pullback_wide_stop_wide_tp
variants.description: Maximum risk buffer variant: 2.5x ATR stop with 5.0x TP (2:1 R:R). If the DD blowout was caused by insufficient stop width, this variant provides the maximum breathing room. At 2:1 R:R with RSI pullback signal quality (PF=1.164 base), even 35% win rate produces positive expectancy. This is the upper bound of the stop width sweep — tests whether MORE room always helps or if there's a point of diminishing returns.
entry_long: EMA_50 slope is rising (EMA_50 > prev EMA_50) confirms intermediate momentum.
RSI_14 <= 42 identifies moderate pullback (slightly tighter than moderate variant for quality).
entry_short: EMA_50 slope is falling (EMA_50 < prev EMA_50) confirms intermediate momentum.
RSI_14 >= 58 identifies moderate rally weakness in the downtrend.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Trend filter: Close must be above/below EMA_200 (structural trend confirmation)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (5.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.5x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: 5.0x TP may rarely be hit on 4h — if win rate drops below 25%, R:R is too wide
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 5.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: RSI_14 pullback — tighter threshold (42) for quality filtering
ATR_14 for widest stop/TP (2.5x/5.0x) — maximum DD buffer

variants.name: rsi_pullback_accept_rr_match
variants.description: Matches the MACD ACCEPT winner's proven R:R ratio (1.0:3.5) but with RSI pullback signal. Tests whether the ACCEPT's R:R is universally optimal or MACD-specific. Stop at 1.5x ATR (minimum safe floor per advisory — NOT 1.0x which caused DD blowout) with 3.5x TP (2.33:1 R:R). This is the tightest safe stop in the sweep.
entry_long: EMA_50 slope is rising (EMA_50 > prev EMA_50) confirms intermediate momentum.
entry_short: EMA_50 slope is falling (EMA_50 < prev EMA_50) confirms intermediate momentum.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Trend filter: Close must be above/below EMA_200 (structural trend confirmation)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (3.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: 1.5x stop is at the advisory minimum floor — if DD > 20%, RSI pullback needs wider stops than MACD
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 3.5x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: RSI_14 shallow pullback detection at 45 threshold
ATR_14 — ACCEPT R:R match (1.5x stop / 3.5x TP)
